notes: LEGACY NAME as of the sensing/segmentation decoupling round: window_size now controls ONLY segment_regions'/to_awn_input's seg_len (segment length == AWN input temporal length T). It no longer controls energy_detect's smoothing window -- that is now --sensing-window-size (see next row), defaulting to window_size when unset so prior behavior is unchanged bit-for-bit (verified: SHA256-identical mask/regions/x_clean vs. the pre-decoupling code path at window_size=128, seed=0). Real 2016.10a checkpoint structurally accepts other EVEN T values (64/128/256/1024 all ran a real forward() pass without error, using dummy all-zero tensors only) because AdaptiveAvgPool1d(1) removes all T-dependence before any Linear layer -- odd T crashes (lifting-scheme even/odd split mismatch). This does NOT mean the checkpoint's classification is valid at non-128 T: it was trained specifically on 128-sample RML2016.10a segments (external/adversarial-rf/util/config.py:51, signal_len=128); no accuracy/statistical validity claim is made for any other length. behavior_test is 'partial' (structural-only, not a claim of correctness)

REVISED this round (md section 11.3): re-tested with a FAIR sweep (single shared attacked IQ generated once per attack, real backend, seed=42, reused across all 4 K values, removing the PGD-randomness confound) -- fgsm 1/4 recovered at K=10 only, pgd 1/2 recovered at K=10 and K=20 only, K=30/40 recovered 0 for both. Recovery trends toward lower K in this sample but is still partial/small-sample -- conclusion unchanged: NOT YET ESTABLISHED as a general claim. Top-K boundary round (md section 12.3, algorithm NOT modified, observation only): topk<=0 (0,-1,-5,-inf) means BYPASS/keep-everything, NOT keep-0-bins (real backend: bit-exact passthrough; dummy: full-bin FFT round-trip, ~2.4e-7 noise, numerically equivalent); topk>T(128) silently clamped to T in both backends (129, 1000000 both -> k=128); topk=1.5 rejected by argparse type=int at CLI (never reaches adapter code); topk=nan: real backend raises uncaught-internally ValueError, TopKAdapter catches it and SILENTLY falls back to dummy (topk_status=fallback, no error surfaced) -- dummy does NOT crash on nan; topk=inf: real raises OverflowError, caught, falls back to dummy, dummy ALSO raises the SAME OverflowError -- uncaught, escapes TopKAdapter.apply() and the whole pipeline (re-confirmed via direct ExperimentConfig(topk=inf)+run_dry_run_experiment, still reproduces). nan/inf/1.5 were all UNREACHABLE via the actual CLI (argparse type=int rejects them at parse time) -- only reachable via direct Python API bypassing argparse. Re-verified via direct ExperimentConfig(topk={inf,nan,1.5})+run_dry_run_experiment: all three now raise a clean ValueError instead of the old uncaught OverflowError.
